steps.title: Identify initial and target concentrations from the graph
steps.content: Read the values directly from the graph. At the initial measurement, $x=0$, the concentration is about $10$ million cells per mL. The question asks how long it takes to reach $20$ million cells per mL.

steps.title: Find the time value where concentration equals 20 million
steps.content: On the graph, move across from $20$ on the concentration axis until you meet the curve, then read down to the time axis. The time is closest to $35$ minutes, so the result is correct.
